vespa 0.4.0

Estimate point cloud normals via PCA

pca_estimate_normals.Rd

Estimates and orients surface normals for an unoriented point cloud using CGAL's PCA-based normal estimation algorithm.

Usage

pca_estimate_normals(
  mesh,
  n_neighbors = 18L,
  orient = TRUE,
  delete_unoriented = TRUE
)

Arguments

mesh

A mesh3d object representing a point cloud (may have an empty $it face matrix).

n_neighbors

Number of nearest neighbours used for local PCA. Default 18L.

orient

Whether to orient the estimated normals consistently. Default TRUE.

delete_unoriented

Whether to remove points whose normals could not be reliably oriented. Default TRUE.

Value

A mesh3d object identical to the input but with an additional $normals field (3×N numeric matrix, one column per point).

See also

poisson_reconstruction()

Examples

# \donttest{
f     <- system.file("extdata", "torus.stl", package = "vespa")
mesh  <- read_stl(f)
cloud <- mesh; cloud$it <- matrix(integer(0), 3L, 0L)
cloud_n <- pca_estimate_normals(cloud)
# }
